title: EU SaaS using US cloud processors
arguments: EU customer data is processed in US-hosted cloud infrastructure and US-based subprocessors. Need transfer-compliant compliance plan for 2026. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU,US --industry saas --strict-facts

title: EU-to-UK transfer scenario
arguments: EU fintech platform transfers limited personal data to UK support operations and needs legally valid transfer posture. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU,UK --industry fintech --strict-facts

title: EU-to-US transfer with DPF plus fallback
arguments: EU SaaS uses US vendors and wants transfer strategy that addresses Schrems II litigation risk and regulator scrutiny. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU,US --industry saas --strict-facts
must_not_include: transfers are automatically compliant without safeguards

title: Intra-EU data residency scenario
arguments: SaaS platform processes data only within the EU and needs a clear statement of transfer obligations and monitoring controls. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU --industry saas --strict-facts
must_not_include: Standard Contractual Clauses required for intra-EU transfers

title: EU data exported to non-adequacy jurisdiction
arguments: EU e-commerce platform exports customer support records to a non-adequacy country through an outsourcing partner and needs a defensible transfer control design. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU,US --industry ecommerce --strict-facts
must_not_include: adequacy decision exists for every recipient country
